question: Did the model output the correct final piece sequence "3, 4, 2, 1" (representing Top-Left, Top-Right, Bottom-Left, Bottom-Right)?
Did the model correctly identify that Image 3 belongs in the top-left position?
Did the model correctly identify that Image 4 belongs in the top-right position?
Did the model correctly identify that Image 2 belongs in the bottom-left position?
Did the model correctly identify that Image 1 belongs in the bottom-right position?
Did the model's reasoning identify that Input Image 3 needs to be rotated (specifically 90 degrees clockwise) to be upright?
Did the model's reasoning identify that Input Image 1 needs to be rotated (specifically 180 degrees) to be upright?
Did the model explicitly mention the split text banner 'COOK OFF!' as a key visual cue for connecting Image 3 and Image 4?
Did the model identify the vertical connection between the Fox's head (Image 3) and the Fox's body (Image 2)?
Did the model identify the vertical connection between the Wolf's head (Image 4) and the Wolf's body (Image 1)?
Did the model correctly identify that the window/shelves (Image 3) and hanging pots (Image 4) represent the upper portion of the scene?
Did the model correctly identify that the cutting board (Image 2) and table ingredients (Image 1) represent the lower portion of the scene?
In the final generated image, is the content of Input Image 3 (Fox head, window) located in the top-left position?
In the final generated image, is the content of Input Image 4 (Wolf head, pots) located in the top-right position?
In the final generated image, is the content of Input Image 2 (Fox body, cutting board) located in the bottom-left position?
In the final generated image, is the content of Input Image 1 (Wolf body, ingredients) located in the bottom-right position?
In the generated image, has the content of Input Image 3 been correctly rotated so that the window/text is upright?
In the generated image, has the content of Input Image 1 been correctly rotated so that the Wolf character is upright?
Does the generated image show a seamless connection for the 'COOK OFF!' banner between the top-left and top-right pieces without severe misalignment?
Do the seams between the connected pieces in the generated image appear visually aligned without unnatural gaps, overlaps, or warping?
If the final reconstructed image is generated, does it depict a coherent kitchen scene with two chef characters (Fox and Wolf) cooking together?
Do the generated images preserve the original visual content of the input pieces without hallucinating significant new elements?
If multiple intermediate images were generated, do pieces that were joined in earlier steps remain in the same relative positions and orientations in subsequent steps?
If the text describes the Fox character as being on the left, does the generated image correctly depict the Fox on the left?
If the text describes the Wolf character as being on the right, does the generated image correctly depict the Wolf on the right?
If the text describes the blue slime/ghost character as being centrally located or between the bottom pieces, does the generated image show this character correctly?
If the text describes a specific spatial relationship (e.g., "Image 3 is above Image 2"), does the generated image accurately reflect this arrangement?
Are there any contradictions where the text claims a specific spatial arrangement or rotation but the generated image shows a different one?
